description: Fresh post-audit migration PRD for agentOS. This backlog supersedes the prior Ralph plan and reopens any work that is still incomplete, regressed, skipped, or only partially verified. The goal is to finish the Rust/kernel migration, remove remaining host-Node guest execution paths, restore agent and command-package parity, and make the full first-party test surface truthful and green.
testPolicy: Treat the old April 7-8, 2026 Ralph progress claims as stale. Use only the exact scoped commands listed in each story. US-001 and US-002 restore reproducible workspace install and normal pnpm/Vitest execution; no TypeScript-package story should be marked complete before those land. The final story requires root `pnpm test`, targeted crate suites (`agent-os-kernel`, `agent-os-bridge`, `agent-os-execution`, `agent-os-v8-runtime`, `agent-os-sidecar`), and the package/registry suites to pass with no first-party ignored Rust tests and no product-debt skips remaining.

userStories.title: Agent integration tests: multi-turn, network, error recovery, multi-agent isolation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive integration tests exercising real agent sessions with mock LLM covering multi-turn conversations, network access, tool error recovery, and multi-agent isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: packages/core/tests/agent-integration.test.ts
Multi-turn: prompt1 writes file → prompt2 reads it back within same session — VFS state persists across turns
Multi-turn: prompt1 sets variable → prompt2 references it — conversation context maintained
Network: agent makes fetch() call during tool execution (e.g., curl a URL) — succeeds through kernel TCP
Error recovery: agent tool gets ENOENT (read nonexistent file) — agent receives error, retries or reports gracefully, session doesn't crash
Error recovery: agent tool gets EACCES (permission denied) — session stays alive
Multi-agent: create Pi session AND Claude session in same VM — each agent's files are visible to both, no interference
Multi-agent: agent A writes /tmp/a.txt, agent B can read it (shared VFS)
All tests use @copilotkit/llmock with mock API keys
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/agent-integration.test.ts passes
userStories.notes: Found in testing gap audit. No existing tests cover multi-turn file state persistence, agent network access during sessions, tool error handling, or multi-agent isolation. These are all scenarios real users will hit.

userStories.title: Agent filesystem edge case tests: large files, binary round-trip, symlinks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests covering VFS edge cases through both exec and host API to catch data corruption and visibility issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: packages/core/tests/agent-fs-edge-cases.test.ts
Large file: write 1MB file via vm.writeFile(), read back via vm.exec('wc -c') — size matches
Large file: write 1MB file via vm.exec('dd ...'), read back via vm.readFile() — size matches
Large file: write 5MB file via vm.writeFile(), verify no silent truncation or ENOSPC without hitting resource limit
Binary round-trip: write 1KB of random bytes via vm.writeFile(), read back via vm.readFile() — bytes identical
Binary round-trip: vm.exec writes binary via printf/xxd, vm.readFile() returns identical bytes
Symlinks: vm.exec('ln -s /tmp/target.txt /tmp/link.txt') then vm.readFile('/tmp/link.txt') follows symlink
Symlinks: vm.writeFile('/tmp/target.txt', 'data') then vm.exec('cat /tmp/link.txt') returns 'data' through symlink
Symlinks: vm.exec('readlink /tmp/link.txt') returns correct target path
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/agent-fs-edge-cases.test.ts passes
userStories.notes: Found in testing gap audit. No existing tests cover large file handling through the VFS cross-layer path, binary file integrity through exec/API round-trips, or symlink visibility across VFS layers. These edge cases are common in real agent workflows (agents write config files, read binary outputs, create symlinks).

userStories.title: Agent lifecycle edge case tests: concurrent sessions, mid-prompt cancellation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests covering session lifecycle edge cases to prevent resource leaks and crashes.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: packages/core/tests/agent-lifecycle.test.ts
Concurrent sessions: create 3 Claude sessions simultaneously, send prompts to all 3, all return responses — no deadlock
Concurrent sessions: resource counts (processes, FDs) return to baseline after all 3 close
Mid-prompt cancel: start a prompt, call closeSession() after 2 seconds — session closes cleanly, no orphaned processes
Mid-prompt cancel: after cancel, creating a new session in same VM works normally
Double close: call closeSession() twice — second call is a no-op, no error
Session after dispose: call closeSession() after vm.dispose() — clear error, no crash
Rapid create/close: create and immediately close 10 sessions in sequence — no resource leak
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/agent-lifecycle.test.ts passes
userStories.notes: Found in testing gap audit. session-cleanup.test.ts exists but only covers basic cleanup. No tests for concurrent sessions under load, mid-prompt cancellation, or rapid create/close cycles. These scenarios happen in production when users create multiple sessions or abort prompts.
